regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are light from the east-southeast at 3 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing smooth embarkation and disembarkation with minimal weather impacts. Port Everglades will see southeast winds at 10 knots and overcast skies today, with a 30% rain probability that may affect shore excursions. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, Miami’s wind shifts to west-southwest at 14 knots with persistent overcast conditions and a 64% rain probability expected to impact port operations and outdoor activities significantly. Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will also experience elevated rain chances and similar wind shifts, which are expected to affect cruise schedules and excursion plans. Beyond 48 hours, Miami’s winds will veer north-northwest at 15 knots with drizzle and a 36% rain probability, suggesting lighter but still notable precipitation that could influence late-stage cruise operations.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, we have light east-southeast winds at 5 knots and clear skies with less than 10% rain probability in Nassau, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions in nearby ports such as Freeport and Bimini. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, south winds will strengthen to 15 knots and a 46% chance of thunderstorms may affect Nassau, Freeport, and the Berry Islands, potentially impacting port operations and excursions. By day three, a 55% rain probability with drizzle and west-northwest winds at 13 knots is forecast, which could affect visibility and dockside activities in Nassau and possibly extend to Key West and Grand Cayman. Passengers and crew should prepare for these changing conditions and monitor updates for any adjustments to scheduled operations.

Passengers and crew should prepare for wet decks today and monitor updates as conditions gradually improve.
